grounding_note: Expansion batch: more relationship-qualification disclosures. Safety-aware wording.

scenarios.opening_query: A man keeps leaving notes on my car and waiting near my bus stop. I need this to stop legally.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's my ex-fiancé — we lived together for two years before I ended it in March.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's my ex-fiancé — two years living together until March.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's a regular from the coffee shop where I work — I know his order and nothing else about him.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's a coffee-shop regular — I know his order and nothing else.
scenarios.vetting_note: Conduct identical; cohabitation history qualifies FAPA.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's father threatened me again and I want a restraining order today.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: I already have the FAPA order — he was arrested for violating it this morning, and the DA's office wants to talk to me about the criminal case today.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I already have the order — he was arrested for violating it this morning and the DA wants to talk to me today.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an order exists and a violation prosecution is underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no order yet — this is the third threat this month and I need the petition filed now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No order yet — third threat this month, and I need the petition filed now.
scenarios.vetting_note: Existing-order-plus-arrest converts the need into criminal-case navigation.

scenarios.opening_query: My elderly father is being hounded by someone who shows up at his apartment demanding to be let in.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Family Law > Restraining Orders || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my brother — Dad's own son — demanding money; they shared the apartment until Dad changed the locks last year.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my brother — Dad's own son, demanding money. They shared that apartment until Dad changed the locks.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the person is a family/household member
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a stranger who 'befriended' him at the senior center and now won't take no — no relation, never lived together.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A stranger from the senior center who won't take no — no relation at all.
scenarios.vetting_note: Family-member harasser qualifies FAPA; stranger doesn't. Elder context adds realism.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone put a tracker on my car. The police found it but say their hands are tied for now.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The receipts trace it to my husband — we're separated, and I'm told that makes it the family-abuse order process.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The receipts trace to my husband — we're separated.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: who placed the tracker and their relationship
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It traces to a coworker I turned down for a date twice — nothing between us, ever.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It traces to a coworker I turned down twice — nothing between us ever.
scenarios.vetting_note: Modern surveillance conduct; identity discovery is naturally late-arriving.

scenarios.opening_query: I moved apartments to get away from someone and they found me within a week.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying
scenarios.hidden_fact: They found me because someone posted my new address in a forum thread dedicated to 'exposing' me — the poster is anonymous and the platform ignores my takedown requests. That's the fight I need help with.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They found me because my new address was posted in a forum thread about 'exposing' me — anonymous poster, and the platform ignores takedowns. That's the fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the operative harm is doxing/platform conduct
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No posting involved — he followed my moving truck. I know exactly who he is and need the stalking order.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No posting — he literally followed my moving truck. I know who he is and need the order.
scenarios.vetting_note: Doxing-vector fact reroutes to the online-harassment label with platform remedies.

scenarios.opening_query: I need protection from my former roommate who has been terrorizing me since I moved out.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We were just roommates from a listing — never partners, no relationship beyond splitting rent, so the advocate says it's the stalking-order statute for me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We were just listing roommates — never partners — so the advocate says the stalking order is my route.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the cohabitation was intimate/domestic or purely economic
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We were together for a year of that lease — the 'roommate' label undersells it, and that relationship qualifies me for the family-abuse order.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We were actually together for a year of that lease — that relationship qualifies me for the family-abuse order.
scenarios.vetting_note: Note: some states treat any cohabitant as FAPA-eligible; the scenario makes the advocate's routing explicit so the intended label is defensible either way.
